rationale: Contract-stability lens on the 17a2ed28..c9f54530 delta. The surface Bead 4 consumes is UNCHANGED: no line touching `func Create`, `type CreateInput`, or any CreateInput field appears in the diff (verified by grep) — the fix is entirely internal marker-scanning helpers, docstring, doc text, and tests. Error semantics stay RunE-usable per ADR-0035: the reworded pair-count error and the NEW whitespace-mangled-marker error are plain fmt.Errorf interpolating only fixed marker constants + integer counts — no target/bead/attacker-controlled byte reaches any Create error string, so Bead 4 can still wrap err.Error() into guard.NewFailure with a recovery line without a newline-forgery vector, and internal/panel still does not import guard. The new corrupt-marker case only WIDENS when Create errors (mangled markers now fail-closed instead of silently prepending a duplicate header) — strictly safer for the consumer, not a contract change. Schema-doc changes keep the Bead-5/111 contract coherent and MORE accurate: the registration paragraph now documents the full-overwrite/revive-abandoned behavior and names the one sanctioned hand-edit (abandon fields) — coherent with Bead 5's Abandon procedure; the verdict-payload wording now matches tally.go's real parsing (enum = what the gate acts on; other non-empty = present-but-inert; empty/whitespace/invalid = MISSING), which is coherence-positive for 111 and does not contradict the BRIEF header's reviewer instruction. The drift test is now section-scoped and still green, with a negative fixture proving a normative rename is caught despite a Maintenance-Notes decoy. My round-1 observation (a) is now documented and pinned by TestCreate_RepanelOfAbandonedPanelRevivesIt. Package green, build green, leaf invariant holds, tree clean.
findings: SIGNATURE/FIELD-SET (unchanged): git diff 17a2ed28..c9f54530 on create.go shows no change to `func Create(dir string, in CreateInput) error` nor to any CreateInput field (BeadID/Spec/Target/Round/ExpectedReviewers/ApproveThresholdExpr/ReviewedHeadSHA). Bead 4's call site is unaffected; the added `regexp` import and new unexported helpers (scanNonFenceLines/findMarkerLines/hasMarkerLookalike/markerLookalikeRE) do not widen the public API.

ERROR SEMANTICS (new cases stay RunE-usable, ADR-0035): the reworded 'expected exactly one genuine ... marker pair' error and the NEW 'found a ... marker mangled by stray whitespace ... fix or remove it by hand' error both interpolate only briefHeaderOpen/briefHeaderClose constants and int counts — no untrusted flag value, so no forged-recovery-line vector when Bead 4 wraps err.Error() into guard.NewFailure. Errors remain plain `error`; the leaf still does not import guard (recovery-line ceremony stays at the CLI adapter). Behavior change is fail-closed on mangled markers — safer for the consumer, not a contract break.

SCHEMA-DOC COHERENCE for Bead 5 (ADDRESSED, coherence-positive): Registration paragraph replaces the inaccurate 'never hand-edited' with steady-state intent + the one sanctioned hand-edit (abandoned/abandon_reason via /ms-panel-tally Abandon) + explicit full-overwrite/revive-abandoned note. This is exactly the boundary Bead 5's retained Abandon procedure needs and resolves my round-1 note (a); the behavior is now pinned by TestCreate_RepanelOfAbandonedPanelRevivesIt.

SCHEMA-DOC COHERENCE for 111 (ADDRESSED): verdict-payload wording now matches tally.go's actual parsing — `verdict` is a non-empty string, reviewers MUST write one of the three enum values (what the gate acts on), any other non-empty string parses present-but-neither-approving-nor-rejecting, empty/whitespace/invalid = MISSING; parsing attributed to panel.Tally and the decision to PanelGateDecision. This does not contradict the (unchanged) BRIEF header's reviewer instruction 'verdict (required): one of APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES/REJECT' — the header tells the reviewer what to write, the doc adds the runner/111-facing parser-tolerance note.

DRIFT PIN (tightened, not loosened): TestPanelSchemaDoc_MatchesConstants now extracts only the '### Panel Artifact Schema' section, and TestPanelSchemaDoc_SectionScoped_RejectsRenamedNormativeName proves a normative `panel.json`->`registration.json` rename inside the section is caught even with a legitimate Maintenance-Notes mention as decoy. The doc-cannot-drift-from-constants guarantee is stronger; all backtick tokens and enum literals still resolve. Both tests pass at c9f54530.

REGRESSION CHECK (approver duty): full `go test ./internal/panel` green (all Create/Tally/Gate/Scan tests), `go build ./...` exit 0, and `deps=$(go list -deps ./internal/panel) && ! grep internal/config` passes — the config-free leaf invariant Bead 4 re-asserts is intact. Bead worktree git status clean after build+test.
